cards.text: What are the three layers (tunics) of a blood vessel wall from innermost to outermost?
cards.answer: Tunica interna (intima)/Tunica intima, Tunica media, Tunica externa (adventitia)

tip.tip: Think of coats — Latin tunica means coat

cards.text: What is the internal elastic lamina and where is it most prominent?
cards.answer: A fenestrated elastic sheet at the intima-media junction; most prominent in muscular arteries/muscular arteries

tip.tip: Key identification feature on histology slides

cards.text: How do elastic arteries differ from muscular arteries in tunica media composition?
cards.answer: 40-70 concentric elastic lamellae in elastic arteries vs 10-40 layers of smooth muscle in muscular arteries/elastic lamellae vs smooth muscle layers
Small blood vessels in the adventitia that supply the outer layers of large vessel walls/vessels that supply the vessel wall

tip.tip: Vessels of the vessels — large arteries need their own blood supply

cards.text: Name the three types of capillaries and a location for each.
cards.answer: Continuous (muscle, lung), fenestrated (kidney glomerulus, intestinal villi), sinusoidal or discontinuous (liver, spleen, bone marrow)/continuous fenestrated sinusoidal

tip.tip: Permeability increases from continuous to sinusoidal

cards.text: What structural feature allows veins to prevent backflow of blood?
cards.answer: Valves — intimal folds with a connective tissue core covered by endothelium/venous valves

tip.tip: Especially important in lower limb veins against gravity

cards.text: Why does an aortic dissection occur in Marfan syndrome?
cards.answer: Defective fibrillin-1 weakens elastic fibres in the tunica media of the aorta, allowing blood to track between layers/fibrillin defect weakens elastic lamellae

tip.tip: The media of elastic arteries is rich in elastic lamellae

cards.text: What is the Windkessel function of elastic arteries?
cards.answer: Elastic arteries expand during systole to store energy and recoil during diastole to maintain continuous blood flow/pressure buffering by elastic recoil

tip.tip: Converts pulsatile flow to more continuous flow

cards.text: What substance does endothelium secrete to cause vasodilation?
In which layer of the arterial wall does atherosclerosis begin?
